input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (previously used in .NET 9).

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated, and .NET MAUI has been moving toward using `WindowInsets` and edge-to-edge display. In .NET 10, they likely completed this transition.

The solution in modern .NET MAUI (.NET 10) involves using the new keyboard/inset handling. 

So the core issue is that .NET MAUI 10 dropped support for the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach because Android deprecated the underlying `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` flag. The fix requires either setting `adjustResize` in the Android manifest directly or properly handling `WindowInsets` with the new edge-to-edge display model that MAUI adopted. itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`.

The modern approach is:
1. Ensure your layout responds to insets (padding/margin adjustments when keyboard appears)
2. Use `KeyboardAccelerator` or handle insets via platform-specific code
3. Or wrap your content in a `ScrollView`

For .NET MAUI on Android, the standard fix involves setting the soft input mode either in the Android manifest or through platform-specific code, and making sure your layout can properly resize when the keyboard appears. If the API was removed, I can work around it by handling keyboard insets manually or using the manifest approach directly. The simplest solution is setting the soft input mode attribute on the activity...
